Why Dharavi slums charge Rs 5 lakh rent a monthThe towers of Mumbai have seen kilometres of expanse of Dharavi's asbestos and tin roofs for decades. Most pity the slums as a place that is deprived of the basics, while others romanticise them. As Asia's largest shanty town stands at the cusp of a major overhaul, it's humming with enterprise. Beyond the filth, Dharavi runs businesses that generate a billion dollars a year.India NewsPM inaugurates India's fastest metro in Meerut, entire Namo Bharat corridorPrime Minister Narendra Modi today launched the remaining portions of the Delhi-Meerut Namo Bharat corridor and the Meerut Metro, India's fastest metro service. The journey on the 82-km RRTS corridor will take just under an hour.India NewsWas critical of PM after Godhra, but Gujarat visit changed views: Bihar GovernorBihar Governor Arif Mohammed Khan said he had criticised then-chief minister of Gujarat Narendra Modi after the 2002 Godhra unrest but changed his view after spending over four months in Gujarat interacting with people from different communities.advertisementHyderabad NewsSpeeding Ferrari loses control, rams multiple cars in Hyderabad; several injuredA speeding Ferrari lost control, jumped onto the wrong side, and rammed multiple vehicles in Hyderabad's Jubilee Hills, injuring several people.How pet travel in India is going paw-sitiveAfter railways made carrying pets less of a hassle, Akasa Air has thrown its cabins wide open for what is now emerging as serious business in travelCricketChasing Virat Kohli, losing themselves: Pakistan stars who faded awayPakistan has produced several cricketers, including Babar Azam, who were compared to Virat Kohli. While many made impressive starts to their careers, most struggled to demonstrate the level of consistency and dedication that Kohli has maintained over the years at the highest level of cricket.T20 World CupThe tale of two Indias: Surya offers rare glimpse into the team's other sideIND vs SA, T20 World Cup: Suryakumar Yadav's Indian team begin their Super 8 campaign against South Africa on Sunday. Here is a rare glimpse into a side of the Indian team that is often unseen, beyond their confident and bullish persona on the global stage. Observations in Ahmedabad, however, suggest a group that is far more grounded, introspective, and measured than their dominant image suggests.SUNDAY SPECIALDarkness redux: Anurag Kashyap is back on the neo-noir trail with KennedyHandcrafted ByOur EditorsAfter over half a decade when he played the field with more mainstream subjects, Anurag Kashyap's new release, Kennedy, marks his return to exploring dark cinema.GURUGRAMSanitiser on private parts, set ablaze: Teen attacked by partner narrates horrorA 19-year-old Tripura woman, who was allegedly attacked by her live-in partner in Gurugram, has alleged that the accused poured sanitiser on her private parts and set it on fire, and assaulted her with a knife for three days.advertisementTRENDING TOPICSNamo BharatSuryakumar YadavTamil Nadu PollsPakistan AirstrikesHoroscope TodayLove HoroscopePlay Puzzle GamesWord!Have you played today’s Word?Sliding  PuzzleMove the tiles to complete the pictureSpot the differenceCan you tell where the two images differ?Word SearchHow many words can you find in the grid?The DailyPlay the daily crosswordWord on WORDDrop the letters strategically to form wordsCelebritiesDhurandhar 2 vs Toxic: RGV makes his choice, calls it a clash between two culturesDirector Ram Gopal Varma reaffirmed support for Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar and its sequel ahead of its highly anticipated box office clash with Yash's Toxic: A Fairytale for Grown-Ups.India NewsUP was in fear earlier, now only development: PM Modi slams past governmentsThe Prime Minister also took a sharp jibe at the Congress and the Samajwadi Party for allegedly spewing venomous politics in the state as well as in the country.India NewsTerrorist killed in J&K's Kishtwar gunbattle, top Jaish commander trappedA terrorist was killed in a gunbattle with security forces in Jammu and Kashmir's Kishtwar.
